title: Cryptographic Agility, Dependencies, FIPS, and Policy Evidence · Runtime Contract: Crypto Go Policy Exception
summary: A compliance document lists approved algorithms but not the executing module, configuration, boundary, certificate status, dependencies, exceptions, or operating procedures. Predict the asset, adversary, security property, trust boundary, and falsifying evidence before opening the governing standard. Planned artifact: Add and verify the policy exception increment in algorithm inventory and compliance evidence packet. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice,…
objectives: Record scoped exception owner, rationale, compensating controls, telemetry, expiry, migration work, approval, and verified closure.
Pin supported Go and x/crypto versions, review release and security notes, run govulncheck, verify reachable findings, and execute compatibility gates.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Predict Crypto Go Policy Exception · Theory Crypto Go Policy Exception
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory cryptographic engineering team handles case 4609 during a certificate rollover incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to bind crypto inventory, Go and module versions, policy enforcement, honest compliance status, and expiring exceptions; reject toy, obsolete, unauthenticated, self-signed-trust, downgrade, and security-by-obscurity shortcuts; then remove one transfer gate and state exactly which production claim is no longer supported. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Crypto Go Policy Exception contract for the supplied data.
